framework.name: CASEL SEL Framework
framework.description: Social and emotional learning (SEL) is the process through which all young people and adults acquire and apply the knowledge, skills, and attitudes to develop healthy identities, manage emotions and achieve personal and collective goals, feel and show empathy for others, establish and maintain supportive relationships, and make responsible and caring decisions.

standards.competency_definition: The abilities to understand one's own emotions, thoughts, and values and how they influence behavior across contexts. Includes recognizing strengths and limitations with a grounded sense of confidence and purpose.

grade_band_progressions.PreK_K: Identify basic emotions; recognize own name and simple preferences
grade_band_progressions.K_2: Name and describe emotions; identify personal strengths
grade_band_progressions.3_5: Understand how emotions affect behavior; recognize personal values
grade_band_progressions.6_8: Analyze complex emotions; reflect on identity and values
grade_band_progressions.9_12: Integrate identity; understand bias; develop purpose and agency

standards.competency_definition: The abilities to manage one's emotions, thoughts, and behaviors effectively in different situations and to achieve goals and aspirations. Includes capacities to delay gratification, manage stress, and feel motivation and agency.

grade_band_progressions.PreK_K: Follow simple routines; manage transitions with support
grade_band_progressions.K_2: Use simple calming strategies; follow classroom rules
grade_band_progressions.3_5: Set short-term goals; manage frustration; persist through challenges
grade_band_progressions.6_8: Set long-term goals; manage stress; demonstrate resilience
grade_band_progressions.9_12: Demonstrate independence; manage complex emotions; pursue goals

standards.competency_definition: The abilities to understand the perspectives of and empathize with others, including those from diverse backgrounds, cultures, and contexts. Includes capacities to feel compassion and understand broader social norms.

grade_band_progressions.PreK_K: Notice feelings of others; show basic empathy
grade_band_progressions.K_2: Recognize emotions in others; understand different perspectives
grade_band_progressions.3_5: Understand others' perspectives; appreciate diversity
grade_band_progressions.6_8: Understand systemic inequities; show compassion across difference
grade_band_progressions.9_12: Analyze societal systems; advocate for equity

standards.competency_definition: The abilities to establish and maintain healthy and supportive relationships and to effectively navigate settings with diverse individuals and groups. Includes communicating clearly, cooperating, and problem solving.

grade_band_progressions.PreK_K: Take turns; share; play alongside peers
grade_band_progressions.K_2: Use kind words; ask for help; work cooperatively
grade_band_progressions.3_5: Resolve conflicts; demonstrate active listening; lead and follow
grade_band_progressions.6_8: Navigate complex social dynamics; mentor peers; advocate
grade_band_progressions.9_12: Build professional relationships; resolve complex conflicts

standards.competency_definition: The abilities to make caring and constructive choices about personal behavior and social interactions across diverse situations. Includes considering ethical standards, safety concerns, and consequences.

grade_band_progressions.PreK_K: Identify safe vs. unsafe behaviors with guidance
grade_band_progressions.K_2: Identify consequences; make simple responsible choices
grade_band_progressions.3_5: Analyze situations; evaluate choices; reflect on outcomes
grade_band_progressions.6_8: Evaluate ethical dilemmas; consider community impact
grade_band_progressions.9_12: Make autonomous ethical decisions; consider long-term impact
